mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 2.5% signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors rotate into equities seeking yield.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS falls below 2.5% and remains there for at least 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 2.4% suggests easing environment
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS is below 2.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and spending, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as investors anticipate earnings downgrades.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points in a single month
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE at 4.0% is near historical lows
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with labor market cooling
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite unemployment spike, or the correlation between UNRATE changes and forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in retail sales (RSAFS) over two consecutive months indicates weakening consumer demand and economic growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as investors reprice earnings expectations downward.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines month-over-month for two consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 58% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth above 1.5% year-over-year combined with inflation (CPIAUCSL) remaining below 3% signals healthy supply-side expansion without overheating, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors gain confidence in sustainable growth.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO year-over-year growth exceeds 1.5% AND CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth is below 3%
evidence_refs: fact:Current INDPRO at 109.4447 and CPIAUCSL at 252.673
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when both conditions are met, or the interaction effect (INDPRO × CPIAUCSL) shows Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerating to below 100,000 new jobs per month combined with rising unemployment signals labor market weakness that precedes equity market selloffs, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month growth falls below 100,000 jobs AND UNRATE rises by more than 0.2 percentage points in the same month
evidence_refs: fact:Current PAYEMS at 150574 thousand persons and UNRATE at 4.0%
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the joint signal (PAYEMS deceleration AND UNRATE rise) shows no significant predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) declining by more than 10% month-over-month signals a contraction in residential investment and forward economic activity, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as investors anticipate broader economic slowdown.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines by more than 10% month-over-month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 57% of triggered instances, or the correlation between HOUST declines and forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) accelerating above 2.5% annualized combined with rising payroll employment signals strong economic momentum that supports equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as investors increase risk exposure.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth exceeds 2.5% AND PAYEMS shows month-over-month growth above 150,000 jobs
evidence_refs: fact:Current GDPC1 at 18664.973 billion chained 2017 dollars and PAYEMS at 150574 thousand
regime_hint: mid-to-late cycle expansion with strong labor market
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances when both conditions are met, or the interaction effect shows Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) rising above 3.5% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains below 3% signals monetary policy lag and real rate compression that creates asset price inflation, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors seek inflation hedges in equities.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth exceeds 3.5% AND FEDFUNDS is below 3%
evidence_refs: fact:Current CPIAUCSL at 252.673 and FEDFUNDS at 2.4%
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 58% of triggered instances, or the real rate compression signal (CPIAUCSL - FEDFUNDS spread) shows Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.
